This implements an ANSI (VT100) terminal emulator as a subclass of screen.

foutr
DoLog
termz
This class is an abstract, generic terminal.
    This does nothing. This is a placeholder that
    provides a common base class for other terminals
    such as an ANSI terminal. 
__init__
selfr'
args
kwargsr
term.__init__N)
__name__
__module__
__qualname__
__doc__rF
ANSIz
This class implements an ANSI (VT100) terminal.
    It is a stream filter that recognizes ANSI terminal
    escape sequences and maintains the state of a screen object. rD
d"d!|
d%t#d

d%t$d

z4Process a single character. Called by :meth:`write`.N)

ANSI.processc
process_list
ANSI.process_listc
zaProcess text, writing it to the virtual screen while handling
        ANSI escape codes.
        N)
ANSI.writec
flush,
ANSI.flushc
This puts a character at the current cursor position. The cursor
        position is moved forward with wrap-around, but no scrolling is done if
        the cursor hits the lower-right corner of the screen. r
crlf

ANSI.write_chc
z&Select Graphic Rendition, e.g. color. r
ANSI.do_sgrc
z'Select character protection attribute. r
ANSI.do_decscac
Handler for 
[?<number>h and 
[?<number>l. If anyone
        wanted to actually use these, they'd need to add more states to the
        FSM rather than just improve or override this method. r
ANSI.do_modecrapN)
